recent_trades.summary: Initiating AVGO position on massive +16.3% 30D momentum driven by CoreWeave/Anthropic AI infrastructure deals.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO is surging on AI infrastructure buildout theme, near 30-day highs at 371 vs 290 low. CoreWeave multibillion Anthropic deal is a direct positive for networking/custom silicon suppliers. Starting at 10% weight.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AMZN weight after Jassy provides harder AI numbers, strong +13.5% 30D momentum, and positive cloud/AI catalysts.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN is at 30-day highs with clear breakout momentum. The Jassy AI disclosure headline is a fundamental positive for AWS. Unrealized gain of 7.7% confirms trend. Moving from ~10% to 15%.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to NVDA on extending winning streak, strong AI demand catalysts, and price approaching 30-day highs with positive sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA is up 6.5% over 30D, just hit 190 (near 30D high), with headlines about longest rally in two years. Anthropic AI capacity deals and TSMC strong March sales confirm AI chip demand boom. Increasing weight from ~10% to 15%.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming WMT to zero as defensive staples underperform and capital is better deployed in AI momentum trades.
recent_trades.reasoning: WMT is slightly negative on unrealized P&L and trading flat/slightly below SMA. With the AI trade firing on all cylinders, rotating out of defensive names to concentrate in higher-momentum positions.

recent_trades.summary: Selling COST at a 3% loss to redeploy into higher-conviction AI momentum names.
recent_trades.reasoning: COST is trading below its 20-day SMA and showing weakness. Consumer staples are underperforming relative to tech/AI names. Freeing up capital for stronger positions. No positive catalysts visible.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting HD to reduce to 10 positions — stock is in a clear downtrend (-11.4% over 30D) with negative sentiment and Jim Cramer calling it one of his worst stocks.
recent_trades.reasoning: HD is below its 20-day SMA, down 11.4% over 30 days, and showing no signs of reversal. Negative news sentiment and weak housing backdrop make this a clear cut. This also fixes the 11-position violation.

recent_trades.summary: Opening 10% INTC position on explosive 37% 30-day rally and AI cloud deal momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC surged 36.8% today with positive 0.30 sentiment, multiple AI-related headlines and a clear technical breakout from 40s lows; target weight respects 20% cap and uses proceeds from LLY sale to capture continued semiconductor strength.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting small LLY position to rotate capital into stronger AI-themed momentum names.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY is only 2% of portfolio with -1.45% unrealized loss and no direct AI catalyst today; selling frees a slot within the 10-position limit while AI/semiconductor stocks show superior technical breakouts and news flow.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating an 8% position in INTC to capture its massive recent momentum and renewed long-term AI cloud prospects.
recent_trades.reasoning: Intel has surged over 36% in the last 30 days, supported by news of its AI cloud deal with Google and broad analyst optimism. Adding a moderate position diversifies our semiconductor exposure while riding the very strong short-term trend.

recent_trades.summary: Sizing NVDA up to the maximum 20% weight to capture its continued winning streak and strong institutional focus on AI capacity.
recent_trades.reasoning: Nvidia is seeing significant positive sentiment and momentum as it eyes its longest rally in two years. The broader ecosystem investments in Anthropic and cloud capacity continue to directly benefit its dominant market positioning.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AMZN to maximum weight following CEO Andy Jassy providing concrete AI revenue numbers that spurred a stock jump.
recent_trades.reasoning: Amazon is displaying excellent momentum, up over 13% in the last 5 days. The fresh fundamental catalyst of quantified AI traction provides high conviction, justifying sizing the position up to the maximum 20% limit to capitalize on the breakout.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting UNH to free up capital for higher-conviction AI and semiconductor opportunities after recent underperformance.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH is currently dragging the portfolio with a negative unrealized return and lacks near-term positive catalysts. Reallocating this capital into strong momentum AI plays optimizes our end-of-day positioning and maximizes capital efficiency.

recent_trades.summary: Topping up AMZN on strong AI-driven news and a momentum push near fresh 30-day highs, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN has one of the strongest combined momentum profiles in the book at +13.64% over 5 days and +13.51% over 30 days, with price closing just under its 30-day high after a breakout run. Positive headlines around AWS/AI disclosure from Jassy strengthen the fundamental narrative, so raising the position from 17.98% to the 20% cap is a high-conviction way to lean into the market's current leadership.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to GOOGL as it breaks back toward its 30-day high with improving short-term momentum, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: GOOGL is up 7.26% over 5 days, sits near its 30-day high of 322.08, and the chart shows a sharp rebound from the recent dip with price now above the 20-day trend line. News flow is neutral-to-supportive, including YouTube pricing power, and increasing the weight from 4.01% to 8% adds to an existing winner while keeping position size moderate versus higher-beta AI holdings.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting UNH to free capital from a weak, non-leading position with limited momentum, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH is a small existing holding with a slight unrealized loss and no supportive news or technical leadership in the provided setup, making it the lowest-conviction slot in a nearly full portfolio. Rotating out of this 3.98% position creates room under the 10-position limit to add to a stronger trend without materially increasing total portfolio risk.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to AMZN as Jassy provides harder AI numbers boosting investor confidence, with stock up 13.5% over 30 days showing strong momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN is up +13.51% over 30 days and +13.64% over 5 days, currently at $238.38 near its 30D high of $240.43. Today's headline 'Amazon stock jumps after Jassy gives investors harder AI numbers' is a significant positive catalyst. Current weight is 10.31%; increasing to 15% adds meaningful conviction to this position. AWS AI infrastructure is a direct beneficiary of the AI spending cycle.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to NVDA on extending winning streak with AI infrastructure demand accelerating — Jassy's hard AI numbers and CoreWeave/Anthropic deals confirm secular tailwind.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA is up +6.5% over 30 days and +6.34% over 5 days, extending its longest rally in two years per today's headlines. Sentiment is strongly positive (+0.41). The CoreWeave-Anthropic multibillion dollar AI capacity deal and TSMC's strong March sales confirming AI chip demand boom all reinforce NVDA's fundamental story. Current weight is 9.92%; increasing to 18% is a conviction add near the 30D high of $190 with strong momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting UNH — small 3.85% position with slight loss, no positive catalysts in news, freeing capacity for higher-conviction AI momentum trades.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH is a small underweight position (-0.54% unrealized) with no meaningful news catalysts today. Healthcare is not showing relative strength versus AI/tech names. Selling this frees up ~4% of portfolio weight and one position slot to deploy into stronger momentum areas where I have higher conviction.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting HD entirely — Jim Cramer flagged it as one of his worst stocks, 30D down 11.4%, and consumer discretionary macro headwinds persist.

The consumer home improvement space faces headwinds from housing market slowdown. Freeing up ~5% allocation to redeploy into stronger momentum names with better AI/tech tailwinds.

Need to reduce from 11 to 10 positions per rules. This is the clearest candidate to cut.

recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN has negative sentiment (-0.15) and is overweight at 15.32% weight; reducing exposure frees cash for better opportunities while maintaining some position given its positive technical performance.

recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO is one of only a few names showing positive price action today (+0.8%), with the headline 'AMD Gains 4%, Broadcom Climbs 5%: The AI Chip Rally Has Two Very Different Winners Today' directly calling out Broadcom strength. The 30D trend is +0.83% with the stock near highs at 374. Sentiment is +0.23. This is a high-quality semiconductor with strong AI exposure. Allocating 15% weight gives meaningful exposure while respecting the 20% cap.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming WMT fully as it's underperforming with -1.4% on the day and -0.52% unrealized loss, with limited upside catalyst in current macro environment.

recent_trades.summary: Selling NVDA on negative sentiment headlines and to free up cash for higher-conviction opportunities, confidence 7/10.

recent_trades.summary: Slightly increasing Alphabet weight following positive developments related to AI partnerships.
recent_trades.reasoning: Recent news surrounding AI deals and Anthropic cloud capacity reinforces Alphabet's cloud narrative. Adding modestly to the existing position to capitalize on the positive idiosyncratic sentiment.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing NVIDIA exposure as it shows continued momentum and serves as a secular AI market leader.
recent_trades.reasoning: Despite negative broader market sentiment, NVIDIA is up significantly today (+1.4%). Its insulation from consumer sentiment metrics and central role in the AI infrastructure buildout make it a preferred defensive growth asset in this environment.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Broadcom on relative strength and positive news regarding institutional accumulation.
recent_trades.reasoning: Broadcom is outperforming today (+0.5%) in a largely negative market, supported by strong AI chip demand and reports of prominent institutional buying. Increasing allocation from 10.5% to 15% captures this momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Walmart to aggressively reduce consumer retail exposure in light of dismal consumer sentiment data.
recent_trades.reasoning: Similar to Costco, Walmart is heavily exposed to a deteriorating consumer outlook. Removing this position completely shifts the portfolio toward cash and secular growth plays that are less reliant on the average consumer's discretionary spending.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Costco entirely due to a record drop in U.S. consumer sentiment and significant daily price weakness.
recent_trades.reasoning: With U.S. consumer sentiment diving to an all-time low amid the ongoing geopolitical conflict, retail names face heavy fundamental headwinds. COST is down 2.2% today, so cutting the 15% position entirely minimizes further downside and frees up capital.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming UNH as bullish Medicare headlines are failing to lift the stock and the intraday chart remains weak, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH has positive sentiment and supportive reimbursement news, but price action is still down on the day and near the bottom of its 30-day range, which is a bearish divergence between news and tape. In a negative macro backdrop with only one trade left today, reducing the position from roughly 8% to 4% preserves capital and raises cash while keeping some exposure in case the healthcare catalyst eventually gains traction.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to CAT on positive sentiment (+0.23), safe dividend narrative, and the AI infrastructure angle highlighted in recent coverage, with technical strength relative to peers.

The safe dividend story adds defensive quality. Currently at 4.93% weight — increasing to 10% makes sense given the relative strength and positive news backdrop. recent_trades.summary: Adding to NVDA as the clear market leader today (+1.4%) with AI demand narrative reinforced by multiple headlines and strong technical momentum near 30-day highs.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA is the top performer in the universe today, breaking higher while most names fall. AI infrastructure demand remains robust per multiple news stories (TSMC beats, CoreWeave-Anthropic deals, Amazon AI secret weapon). Already up +3.52% unrealized. Increasing weight from ~9.9% to 18% capitalizes on the strongest momentum name with a clear fundamental catalyst.

The stock is only up +0.2% today. Trimming to 5% reduces exposure while keeping some financial sector allocation. This frees ~2.7% of portfolio weight for better opportunities.

universe.description: 75-name universe: top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks by market cap with full GICS sector coverage (3-5+ names per sector).
